title: Share of people who have depressive disorders, by age
subtitle: Estimated share of people who have  [depressive disorders](#dod:depressive_disorders), whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.
caption: In 2023, the estimated share of the global population with depressive disorders varies significantly across age groups, ranging from under 1% to over 5%. The horizontal bar chart displays these prevalence rates, with the lowest value appearing for children aged 5 to 14 at 0.98%. Prevalence rises to 4.55% for the 15 to 19 age group and continues to increase through early adulthood, reaching 4.78% for ages 20 to 24 and 4.91% for ages 25 to 29. The rate hits 5% for the 30 to 34 cohort and remains above 5% for all subsequent age brackets up to 70 and older. The highest prevalence occurs in the 65 to 69 age group at 5.46%, followed closely by the 60 to 64 group at 5.4% and the 70 and older group at 5.36%. Rates for the 55 to 59, 50 to 54, 45 to 49, 40 to 44, and 35 to 39 age groups cluster between 5.14% and 5.39%. The age-standardized estimate stands at 3.81%, while the figure for all ages combined is 4%. The data shows a distribution where prevalence is lowest in childhood, rises through young adulthood, and remains elevated across middle and older age.
